  • P101 — CollabSim: A CSCW-Grounded Methodology for Investigating Collaborative Competence of LLM Agents through Controlled Multi-Agent Experiments [#114, Innovative]
  • P193 — Agentic Environment Engineering for Large Language Models: A Survey of Environment Modeling, Synthesis, Evaluation, and Application [#158, Moderately Innovative]
  • P107 — Research on the Architecture and Implementation of Power Archive Information Platform Based on International and Local Standards [#200, Incremental]
  • P059 — Agentic AI Systems: Architecture, Applications, and a Case Study of OpenClaw [#232, Routine]

Agent Knowledge, Reasoning & Problem-Solving — avg: 49 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers collectively demonstrate the diversification of agent architectures and their application across complementary domains. While P111 leverages RAG-based multi-agent systems for developer onboarding and P156 employs tool-augmented agents with verifiable citation retrieval for misinformation detection, both establish that structured tool integration improves reliability in specialized contexts. P158 extends this principle by replacing flat prompt-based tool retrieval with graph-scaffolded planning over biomedical capability graphs, suggesting that hierarchical structure enhances agent reasoning across complex tool ecosystems. Training approaches vary significantly: P181 focuses on synthesizing shortcut-resistant search tasks to train robust deep search agents, while P204 addresses long-horizon research through multi-agent orchestration with supervised fine-tuning, reflecting divergent strategies for scaling agent capabilities. P191 introduces an orthogonal perspective—that agent capability emerges primarily from environment engineering rather than workflow prescription—providing a counterpoint to the tool-integration-focused methods in the other papers. Collectively, these works chart a trajectory from task-specific tool augmentation toward domain-general agent architectures that leverage hierarchical planning, environment design, and orchestration for complex, long-horizon scientific and research tasks.
  • P181 — FORT-Searcher: Synthesizing Shortcut-Resistant Search Tasks for Training Deep Search Agents [#6, Highly Innovative]
  • P204 — SearchSwarm: Towards Delegation Intelligence in Agentic LLMs for Long-Horizon Deep Research [#32, Innovative]
  • P158 — Beyond Prompt-Based Planning: MCP-Native Graph Planning-based Biomedical Agent System [#127, Moderately Innovative]
  • P191 — EUREKAGENT: Agent Environment Engineering Is All You Need for Autonomous Scientific Discovery [#143, Moderately Innovative]
  • P111 — Creating an Onboarding Tool for Technical Integration Using an LLM Approach [#213, Incremental]
  • P156 — Verifiable Misinformation Detection Using Tool-Augmented Large Language Model Agents [#233, Routine]

LLM Routing & Dynamic Model Selection — avg: 48 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers select the most suitable model or expert for each query. P076 uses feedback-driven entropy (GEP) to route among 70+ heterogeneous local LLMs. P077 uses response-conditioned uncertainty to decide between fast and slow reasoning paths. P086 decomposes queries into structured capability profiles and matches against historical logs. P123 infers user cost-performance preferences via meta-learning from pairwise comparisons. P128 trains a lightweight gating model to dynamically weight multiple specialist LLM logits. P146 routes between thinking and non-thinking inference modes. The trend evolves from static scoring → implicit preference learning → dynamic logit-level fusion.
  • P086 — Beyond Query Memorization: Large Language Model Routing with Query Decomposition and Historical Matching [#98, Innovative]
  • P123 — Learning to Route LLMs from Implicit Cost-Performance Preferences via Meta-Learning [#148, Moderately Innovative]
  • P128 — DLLG: Dynamic Logit-Level Gating of LLM Experts [#151, Moderately Innovative]
  • P077 — When to Think Deeply: Inhibitory Deliberation for LLM Reasoning [#156, Moderately Innovative]
  • P146 — SynapseRoute: an auto-route switching framework on dual-state large language model for medical question answering [#176, Moderately Innovative]
  • P076 — Entropy-Guided Model Routing: A Production Implementation of GEP-Based Dynamic Selection Across Heterogeneous LLM Pools [#215, Incremental]

Agent Governance, Pre-Deployment & Trustworthiness — avg: 45 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers address governance, assurance, and trustworthiness of deployed agents. P125 introduces execution-gated inference, where generative LLM runtimes are instantiated only after pre-execution authorization confirms institution-approved sources exist. P144 evaluates and trains "budget awareness" in agents—the ability to estimate mid-execution remaining resource requirements, expressed as calibrated intervals or impossibility declarations. P157 introduces TrustModel, a conceptual multi-agent platform for automatically generating and continuously evolving knowledge models used in model-driven engineering. P160 proposes a pre-deployment verification framework combining formal Agent Operational Envelopes, ontology-driven test-scenario generation, and cryptographically-bound Trust Certificates with graduated deployment gates. P162 introduces the Autonomous State Control Plane (ASCP), a reference architecture that interposes a deterministic governance layer between probabilistic AI reasoning and real-world system execution. Collectively, these establish institutional control mechanisms: from source verification → resource estimation → knowledge governance → formal assurance → architectural sovereignty.
  • P144 — Towards Budget-Aware Agents: Do LLM Agents Know What They Will Spend? [#27, Innovative]
  • P160 — Toward Pre-Deployment Assurance for Enterprise AI Agents: Ontology-Grounded Simulation and Trust Certification [#159, Moderately Innovative]
  • P157 — Agentic Generation and Evolution of Knowledge Models [#194, Incremental]
  • P125 — COMPAiSS White Paper | Execution-Gated Inference: A Structural Solution to AI Hallucination in Regulated Institutional Environments [#201, Incremental]
  • P162 — The Autonomous State Control Plane: A Reference Architecture for Sovereign AI Systems [#209, Incremental]

Knowledge Graphs & Ontologies — avg: 45 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers collectively demonstrate an evolution in knowledge graph and ontology applications from static semantic structures toward dynamic, AI-integrated systems across diverse domains. P003 and P062 focus on traditional ontology design and RDF authoring—P003 through modular OWL composition for product passports and P062 via AI-assisted mapping from JSON data—while P092 applies similar structured frameworks to construction project management and P006 extends these methods to biochemical pathway modeling for biomanufacturing. Conversely, P005 and P173 showcase increasingly sophisticated integrations of knowledge graphs with deep learning, where P005 employs a five-module pipeline for IoT semantic interoperability and P173 combines GAT-based graph embeddings with GRU attention for library recommendation. Bridging these approaches, P008 surveys embedding-based ontology extensions and proposes hybrid quantum-fuzzy systems to reconcile probabilistic and crisp inference, while P012 uses knowledge graphs as soft data-overridable priors in Bayesian structure learning. Finally, P174 introduces Type-2 Neutrosophic ontologies with uncertainty quantification for automated essay scoring, suggesting that the field is moving toward frameworks that encode both symbolic structure and probabilistic confidence measures. Together, these papers reveal a field transitioning from static knowledge representation toward ontologies and graphs that actively guide and constrain neural learning systems, accommodating uncertainty and domain specificity.
  • P012 — KG-SoftMAP: Soft Knowledge-Graph Priors for Bayesian Network Structure Learning from Sparse Discrete Data [#35, Innovative]
  • P174 — Intelligent automated essay scoring under uncertainty using type 2 neutrosophic ontologies [#149, Moderately Innovative]
  • P173 — Smart Library Personalized Resource Proactive Recommendation System Integrating User Profiling and Knowledge Graphs [#164, Moderately Innovative]
  • P062 — MetaConfigurator: AI-Assisted RDF Authoring from JSON Data [#184, Moderately Innovative]
  • P006 — A Biological Systems-of-Systems Ontology for Multi-Scale Biomanufacturing Process Modeling and Simulation [#185, Moderately Innovative]
  • P003 — Modular Ontology Design Approach for an ESPR Compliant Digital Product Passport Core Ontology [#188, Moderately Innovative]
  • P092 — Ontology-Based Framework for Construction Cost Management Using BIM for Small-Scale Public Infrastructure Projects [#190, Incremental]
  • P005 — Design of an improved validation model for semantic interoperability in IoT using MOVFRGD ECDTSA and CCEAICM process [#197, Incremental]
  • P008 — Extending Ontologies: From Dense Embeddings to Hybrid Quantum-Fuzzy Systems [#205, Incremental]

Computational Analysis & Multi-case Studies — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

P014 applies Callon mapping and Multiple Correspondence Analysis to 706 tourism-environment papers, uncovering a 47.59% Dimension 1 bifurcation between adaptation and carbon-accounting streams plus five structurally absent research directions; P055 decomposes four event-log time series via STL, finding vf(Seasonal)>0.30 predicts 24.03% RMSE improvement in downstream ARIMA/SARIMA and identifying counter-intuitive negative correlations; P061 runs twenty cyber-range experiments with five LLM agents in distinct APT profiles, discovering technique convergence at early kill-chain phases and undocumented Velociraptor C2 weaponization (8/10 scenarios) with 55–80% MITRE ATT&CK precision; P113 meta-analyzes twenty-two historical shocks using per-shock OLS regression, finding positive lobbying effects only for regulatory shocks (p=0.046) versus non-regulatory or systemic shocks (both p>0.1). All four apply computational or statistical methods across multiple datasets/experiments (4–706 units) to extract empirical discoveries rather than feasibility proofs. They use established techniques (dimensionality reduction, decomposition, agent replication, meta-regression) on real or simulated data to produce quantitative findings beyond visual inspection. Collectively they demonstrate that scaling analysis beyond toy examples reveals patterns (dimensional structure, forecastability conditions, convergent behaviors, shock-dependent effects) invisible in smaller studies, though rigor varies across workshop, proprietary, and dissertation contexts.
  • P061 — Synthetic APTs: the Collapse of TTP-Based Attribution [#144, Moderately Innovative]
  • P055 — Making Sense of Continuous Process Dynamics [#171, Moderately Innovative]
  • P113 — Does Lobbying Pay in Times of Crisis? A Coordination Perspective on Lobbying [#186, Moderately Innovative]
  • P014 — Tourism and Environmental Impact: A Bibliometric Analysis of Two Decades of Research [#208, Incremental]

Healthcare & Medical Data Systems — avg: 43 (Moderately Innovative)

This cluster centers on building standardized data systems and evaluation frameworks across healthcare and educational domains. P002 provides infrastructure for federated healthcare deployment through OMOP partitioning, while P051 establishes a unified benchmarking framework for synthetic EHR generation—both addressing reproducibility in medical data systems. P106 shifts to computational reasoning, using multi-agent aggregation to predict drug-target interactions from biomedical evidence. Meanwhile, P109 and P153 extend these systematic, data-driven approaches to adjacent domains: P109 applies deep learning and reinforcement learning to assess English language proficiency, while P153 presents a cloud-native pipeline for educational data validation. Collectively, these papers suggest a broader trend toward modular, standardized systems that combine infrastructure, evaluation, and intelligent prediction across healthcare and educational contexts.
  • P051 — Accelerating Reproducible Research in Synthetic EHR Generation [#102, Innovative]
  • P106 — DrugAgent: Reliable Multi-Agent Aggregation under Conflicting Biomedical Evidence [#170, Moderately Innovative]
  • P109 — Deep Learning-Based Intelligent Diagnosis and Adaptive Training System for University English Oral Proficiency [#191, Incremental]
  • P153 — POWERING AN ECOSYSTEM OF PEDAGOGICAL AI AGENTS: A VALIDATION STRATEGY FOR A UNIFIED DATA ARCHITECTURE [#202, Incremental]
  • P002 — A Patient-Centric OMOP-CDM Partitioning Tool for Federated Deployment Testing [#206, Incremental]

Education & Learning Systems — avg: 43 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers span diverse applications across education and organizational systems, yet converge on data-driven prediction and analytics. P108 and P168 both address core educational outcomes—grade prediction and knowledge tracing respectively—using machine learning, while P071 applies similar predictive techniques to employee retention in a government organization. However, the approaches diverge significantly: P108 uses adaptive clustering and ensemble methods, P168 focuses on explainability through SHAP audits that reveal when additional features degrade model performance, and P071 integrates Random Forest predictions with dashboard visualization for decision-making. P033 takes a distinct qualitative, survey-based approach to assess school farms' entrepreneurial role within tertiary institutions. Collectively, these papers suggest a field moving toward predictive analytics in learning and organizational systems, with increasing sophistication in model selection (P108's cluster-based specialists), explainability and auditing practices (P168's calibration analysis), and practical integration into existing workflows (P071's HRIS dashboards), while foundational qualitative assessment of educational interventions remains valuable (P033).
  • P108 — Predicting student grades via adaptive multi-level learning models [#74, Innovative]
  • P168 — An Explainable Calibration and SHAP Audit of Help-Seeking Features in Classical Knowledge Tracing [#85, Innovative]
  • P071 — Employee Retention Prediction for the National Economic and Development Authority Using Decision Trees for Advanced HR Analytics with Data Visualization [#210, Incremental]
  • P033 — Establishment of School Farms: Assessing Its Roles in Developing Entrepreneurial Ventures for Public Tertiary Institutions in Akwa Ibom State [#229, Routine]

Agent Multi-Agent Coordination & Delegation — avg: 41 (Moderately Innovative)

P046 and P161 both tackle multi-agent coordination challenges but apply them to distinctly different domains: P046 addresses passenger information systems in public transport through a domain-decomposed architecture of five specialized agents aligned with CRISP-DM methodology, while P161 targets software engineering workflows through DAG-based scheduling with dual validation gates and a Human-as-an-Agent framework. Both papers recognize that complex domain problems benefit from task specialization and orchestration—P046 achieves this through functional decomposition (Orchestrator, Data, Analytics, Knowledge, Insights agents), whereas P161 implements it via topological wave scheduling and explicit validation metrics (20 dimensions). The contrast between their approaches suggests an emerging trend in multi-agent design: domain-specific problems require not just agent specialization but also appropriate coordination mechanisms (CRISP-DM pipeline for data-heavy systems, DAG scheduling for sequential engineering tasks). Together, P046 and P161 highlight that the multi-agent paradigm is advancing beyond simple agent multiplication toward intelligent orchestration, validation, and human-in-the-loop integration as differentiators across distinct application domains.
  • P161 — SPOQ: Specialist Orchestrated Queuing for Multi-Agent Software Engineering [#116, Innovative]
  • P046 — Agentenbasierte Fahrgastinformationssysteme für den ÖPNV [#217, Incremental]

Industrial, Manufacturing & Business Operations — avg: 36 (Incremental)

These papers apply AI to industrial systems, manufacturing, and business process optimization. P004 proposes a MILP lifecycle cost framework for deploying Digital Twin technology in gas distribution networks under wartime constraints. P021 measures operational efficiency impact of Oracle ASCP and Demantra MRP deployment at a Finnish manufacturing site via KPI analysis. P087 develops a Contingent Pathways Model identifying four adoption archetypes for BIM-enabled Digital Twins in AECO operations and maintenance. P088 surveys architectural transition from hierarchical (ISA-95, RAMI 4.0) to cloud–edge–device industrial CPS paradigms. P100 combines Colored Petri Net formal modeling with transformer encoder-decoder RL feedback for adaptive job scheduling in flexible/reconfigurable manufacturing. P110 introduces DiTSE, a four-stage design-science framework for incrementally transforming static BIM into semantically enriched, IoT-connected, cyber-physically controllable digital twins. P115 deploys a production LLM-based agent system for automating insurance contract error resolution at Alan, paired with ops-autonomy tooling. P145 simulates three business-model innovation pathways (revenue diversification, digital transformation, green integration) for coal-fired power plants via system dynamics. P147 proposes BP-LSSVM and GA-FARM for minimizing combined denitrification and logistics costs in coal boiler units. P164 integrates deep learning, generative AI, RL, and constraint programming into discrete-event business process simulation for decision support. The trajectory spans strategic infrastructure roadmaps → operational efficiency measurement → adoption pathway modeling → architectural transitions → scheduling optimization → BIM enrichment → contract automation → business model simulation → cost optimization.
  • P164 — AI-Enhanced Business Process Simulation for Decision Making Support [#37, Innovative]
  • P115 — Automating Alan's Internal Processes [#150, Moderately Innovative]
  • P145 — Simulating Business Model Innovation for Sustainable Coal-Fired Power Plants: A Dynamic Business Modeling Approach [#189, Moderately Innovative]
  • P087 — BIM-enabled Digital Twins for Operation and Maintenance in the AECO Industry: A Multiple Case Study [#192, Incremental]
  • P021 — Evaluating the Impact of Deployed Material Requirement Planning (MRP) Tools on Operational Efficiency in Manufacturing and Order Fulfilment Environment: A Case Study Approach [#198, Incremental]
  • P110 — How to Develop More Functional and Future-Ready Digital Twins: Towards a Framework for Digital Twin Scope Evolution [#207, Incremental]
  • P100 — Generative AI-Driven Optimization in Flexible and Reconfigurable Manufacturing Systems [#212, Incremental]
  • P147 — Data-driven Least-squares Support Vector Machine Model for Integrating Wireless Sensor Data in Logistics Optimization and Reducing Denitrification Cost in Thermal Power Generation [#219, Incremental]
  • P088 — Transition of Architecture of Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems (iCPS) from Hierarchy to Cloud-Edge-Device [#223, Incremental]
  • P004 — Strategic Planning and Economic Assessment of the Implementation of Digital Twin Technology in Gas Distribution Network Management Systems [#225, Routine]

Qualitative Research & Stakeholder Input — avg: 35 (Incremental)

P037 uses six semi-structured interviews (five pro-SMR) to map legitimacy-building strategies for a Swedish nuclear startup, surfacing concrete structural barriers (municipal veto, Paris Convention liability); P060 aggregates eighteen expert Delphi surveys via AHP-FAHP to quantify supply-chain-finance risk hierarchies in fast fashion, finding agility risk outweighs credit risk; P094 documents a single fermentation protocol and collects acceptability ratings from thirty local participants despite framing an agro-tourism narrative with zero tourism data; P142 interviews six maritime professionals about AI adoption barriers with no observed deployments. All four rely on structured input from human experts/practitioners without observing deployed systems or independently validated outcomes. Sample sizes are uniformly small (N=6–30), participant selection purposive, and findings descriptive-exploratory rather than causal. P060 applies formal quantification (AHP-FAHP weights) but lacks empirical validation against real outcomes. Collectively they map stakeholder landscapes and perceived benefits while acknowledging severe generalizability constraints; their value lies in hypothesis-generation and context-specific insight rather than transferable evidence.
  • P060 — Multi-Dimensional Assessment of Supply Chain Finance Risks in Fashion Textile and Leather: ESG Integration and Application of AHP-FAHP Method [#181, Moderately Innovative]
  • P037 — Positioning a New Startup in Sweden's Nuclear Landscape: A Case Study of Blykalla [#195, Incremental]
  • P142 — AI-Powered Workflow Automation in Finnish Maritime Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises [#199, Incremental]
  • P094 — Sustainable dragon fruit wine as an agro-tourism product: Implications for local tourism systems and community-based commercialization [#222, Incremental]

Conceptual Frameworks & Visions — avg: 33 (Incremental)

P020 proposes a four-layer network taxonomy and quantum-metaphor framing for defense acquisition to capture cross-domain coupling; P074 derives organizational necessity from post-AI resource shifts and introduces the mixer-mode cognitive construct grounded in cognitive science; P170 sketches microservices/GraphQL/ontology integration architecture for disaster systems; P177 proposes homoiconic substrates coupling LLMs with deterministic symbolic environments. All four identify genuine structural problems (acquisition silos, organizational misalignment with agentic AI, system interoperability, LLM-determinism tension) and propose architectural solutions without implementation or measurement. They function as research agendas informed by adjacent domains (quantum physics, organizational theory, software engineering, biology) rather than validated contributions, with P074 and P170 most explicit about pre-empirical status. Collectively they show how conceptual reframing can clarify complex institutional or technical problems while deferring the empirical validation burden to future work.
  • P074 — The Two Pillars: A Structural Theory of Post-AI Software Work [#132, Moderately Innovative]
  • P020 — Quantum Acquisition: A New Paradigm for Understanding the Interdependencies of Complex Networks in the Acquisition Life Cycle of Warfighting Systems [#211, Incremental]
  • P177 — The Biomimetic Architecture of Software 4.0 [#216, Incremental]
  • P170 — Methods of Interoperability and Integration of Disaster Management Systems [#220, Incremental]

Large-Scale Generative Systems Integration — avg: 28 (Incremental)

P198 and P159 both grapple with integrating heterogeneous components into unified large-scale systems—P198 through a pipeline combining satellite conditioning, 3D reconstruction, and hierarchical LOD inference deployed globally, and P159 through a conceptual framework integrating AI, analytics, blockchain, and cybersecurity into enterprise architecture. While P198 is a working system with empirical coverage evidence and P159 is unvalidated theory, both identify component integration and cross-domain orchestration as the core challenge at scale.
  • P198 — ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model [#182, Moderately Innovative]
  • P159 — Autonomous Decision Intelligence for Secure and Resilient Digital Enterprises [#231, Routine]

Empirical Demonstrations at Limited Scale — avg: 22 (Incremental)

P010 deploys a MERN-stack prototype with zero evaluation metrics beyond two UI screenshots; P058 runs a forty-request toy test with asymmetric hardware and mixed measurement denominators; P069 optimizes soft-error rates on the ten smallest benchmark circuits, achieving 23.7% reduction but underperforming cited prior methods; P103 runs three LLM models against six software products without ablating the sitemap constraint or testing unconstrained baselines. All four present concrete artifacts (prototype, test runs, circuit results, LLM outputs) on minimal scales—single batch, single test, ten circuits, six products—too small to claim generality. Baselines are absent (P010), misleading (P058), underperformed-but-unmentioned (P069), or never tested (P103). Validation is visual/narrative, toy-scale, benchmark-endemic, or heterogeneous-ground-truth. None claims to advance state-of-art; instead, they function as feasibility proofs or proof-of-concept measurements that stop well short of evidence for performance superiority or reproducibility.
  • P103 — AI-Driven Software Pricing: An Integrated Approach with Prompt Engineering for Market Analysis [#183, Moderately Innovative]
  • P010 — InstaHang: A Proximity-Aware Social Matching System with Hybrid Compatibility Scoring [#224, Incremental]
  • P058 — Research of Fault Tolerance Strategies in Multi-Cloud Environments [#228, Routine]
  • P069 — Deep learning-based prediction of PFAS toxicity in zebrafish [#230, Routine]
